Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Women freedom

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-10-26 · 5 answers
Hare krishna
What do vaishnavas think abt women freedom?

Should women be really allowed to work equally with women?
user [154] · 2010-10-27
I see no difficulty in that [quote][cite] onkar:[/cite]

Should women be really allowed to work equally with women?[/quote]
user [449] · 2010-10-31
I am sorry I meant that

1. Why were women not given so much freedom in vedic culture?
2. What did SP say about women freedom?
3. I heard in one class in ISKCON that women are spiritually less intelligent? Can somebody elaborate on this?
user [154] · 2010-11-01
Usually women are dependent in vedic culture. But there are some independent in Mahabharata. Obviously both women and men are spiritually less intelligent. Spiritually more intelligent are those who do not identify with man or woman tag.

See also:

http://www.bbt.info/information/toughones/kalakanthaonwomen
http://www.bbt.info/information/toughones/womantodo
user [447] · 2010-11-01
Heres a discussion on this topic at Dandavats: http://www.dandavats.com/?p=600
user [38] · 2010-11-01
http://veda.harekrsna.cz/library/Women.zip
